label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's analyze this question about causation. I need to determine if the nurse's carelessness caused John's premature death based on the details provided. Let me start by clearly understanding the sequence of events and the key facts. John's background: - 17 years old when he started at a manufacturing company - Worked on the assembly line for a few years - Given a choice between two positions: stay in woodwork division or switch to plastics division - Decided to switch to plastics division to try something new - For the last 30 years, worked as a production line supervisor in plastics division - After first year in plastics division, the division was moved to a different building with more space - In the plastics division, John was exposed to asbestos, a highly carcinogenic substance - The small part of the plastics division where John worked had asbestos exposure - John has never smoked and otherwise has a healthy lifestyle - At age 50, John has a highly progressed, incurable case of lung cancer - Seen by three specialists who confirmed: cancer is untreatable except for pain, John is certain to die soon (within 2 months) - Yesterday, while in the hospital for a routine appointment, a new nurse accidentally gave him the wrong medication - John is allergic to the drug and went into shock and had cardiac arrest - Doctors tried to resuscitate him but failed - John died minutes after the medication was administered Now, the question is whether the nurse's carelessness caused
